---
title: "can't get dual monitors to work"
date: 2006-08-04
forum: General Help
---

### Post by paperdiesel on 2006-08-04
So I've done extensive reading (on these forums and on others) trying to get my set up to work.  Breaks down like this:

I have two video cards and two monitors.  I want to have both monitors on at the same time.  I DO NOT want xinerama nor twinview.  I just want two monitors with two distinct desktops.  I know I'm close, but something in my xorg.conf file is not right.  Here's the file:

```
# nvidia-xconfig: X configuration file generated by nvidia-xconfig
# nvidia-xconfig:  version 1.0  (buildmeister@builder3)  Mon May 15 13:23:42 PDT 2006

# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the /etc/X11/xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man /etc/X11/xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)

# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "ServerLayout"
  Identifier "Default Layout"
  screen 0 "nvidia_screen"
  screen 1 "sis_screen" leftof "nvidia_screen" 
  InputDevice "Generic Keyboard"
  InputDevice "Configured Mouse"
EndSection

Section "Files"
  
  # path to defoma fonts
  FontPath "/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"
  FontPath "/usr/share/X11/fonts/cyrillic"
  FontPath "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi/:unscaled"
  FontPath "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi/:unscaled"
  FontPath "/usr/share/X11/fonts/Type1"
  FontPath "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi"
  FontPath "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi"

  option "XkbLayout" "us"

    modes "1280x1024@75" "1280x960@60" "1280x854" "1280x960@85" "1152x768@54" "1280x1024@60" "1152x864@75" "1280x960@75" "1024x768@43" "1400x1050@60" "1024x768@60" "1400x1050@75" "1024x768@70" "1600x1200@65" "1024x768@75" "1600x1200@60" "1024x768@85" "1600x1200@70" "832x624@75" "1792x1344@60" "800x600@60" "1856x1392@60" "800x600@85" "800x600@75" "800x600@72" "800x600@56" "640x480@85" "640x480@75" "640x480@72" "640x480@60"
```

What happens is my main screen (nvidia) displays fine.  The sis screen is blank and the monitor's light is off.  What am I doing wrong?

---

### Post by misterE0 on 2006-08-04
as far as i know, if you want two monitors, you must use either twinview or xinerama.  Can I ask why you don't want twinview?  If want you're looking to do is get your desktop to span two monitors, then twinview is what you want. if you mean two seperate x sessions, i'm not sure if that's even possible on one vid card.

---

### Post by paperdiesel on 2006-08-04
> **misterE0 said:**
> as far as i know, if you want two monitors, you must use either twinview or xinerama.  Can I ask why you don't want twinview?  If want you're looking to do is get your desktop to span two monitors, then twinview is what you want. if you mean two seperate x sessions, i'm not sure if that's even possible on one vid card.

I don't want either twinview nor xinerama because it breaks my gaming.  I know it's possible to have two monitors (note this is the SAME xsession, just two desktops).  If I take the "screen" declaration out of my "Device" sections, I get both monitors working.  The problem there is that both monitors share the same resolution, which is bad.  The left monitor needs to be 1024 x 768 while the right monitor needs to be 1280 x 1024.

---

### Post by misterE0 on 2006-08-05
at least part of the problem is that you don't specify any resolution for your second monitor.  Take a look at the MetaModes section in my xorg.conf:

```

# nvidia-xconfig: X configuration file generated by nvidia-xconfig
# nvidia-xconfig:  version 1.0  (buildmeister@builder3)  Mon May 15 13:23:42 PDT 2006

# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the /etc/X11/xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man /etc/X11/xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg
#Section "InputDevice"
#	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
#	Driver		"mouse"
#	Option		"CorePointer"
#	Option		"Device"		"/dev/input/mice"
#	Option		"Protocol"		"ExplorerPS/2"
#	Option		"ZAxisMapping"		"4 5"
#	Option		"Buttons"		"7"
#	Option		"Emulate3Buttons"	"true"
#EndSection
#Section "InputDevice"
#	Identifier    "Configured Mouse"
#	Driver        "mouse"
#	Option        "Protocol"        "evdev"
#	Option        "Dev Name"        "Logitech USB Gaming Mouse"
#	Option        "Dev Phys"	"usb-0000:00:02.0-1/input0"  
#	Option        "Device"        	"/dev/input/event1"
#	Option        "Buttons"        	"10"
#	Option        "ZAxisMapping"	"4 5"
#EndSection
#Section "InputDevice"
#	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
#	Driver		"evdev"
#	Option		"CorePointer"
#	Option		"Device"	"/dev/input/event1"
#EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
    Identifier     "Default Layout"
    Screen         "Default Screen" 0 0
    InputDevice    "Generic Keyboard"
    InputDevice    "Configured Mouse"
    InputDevice    "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
    InputDevice    "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
    InputDevice    "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"
EndSection

Section "Files"

	# path to defoma fonts
    FontPath        "/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"
    FontPath        "/usr/share/X11/fonts/cyrillic"
    FontPath        "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi/:unscaled"
    FontPath        "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi/:unscaled"
    FontPath        "/usr/share/X11/fonts/Type1"
    FontPath        "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi"
    FontPath        "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi"
    FontPath        "/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
EndSection

Section "Module"
    Load           "i2c"
    Load           "bitmap"
    Load           "ddc"
    Load           "extmod"
    Load           "freetype"
    Load           "glx"
    Load           "int10"
    Load           "type1"
    Load           "vbe"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    Identifier     "Generic Keyboard"
    Driver         "kbd"
    Option         "CoreKeyboard"
    Option         "XkbRules" "xorg"
    Option         "XkbModel" "pc104"
    Option         "XkbLayout" "us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    Identifier     "Configured Mouse"
    Driver         "evdev"
    Option         "CorePointer"
    Option         "Device" "/dev/input/g5mouse"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
    Identifier     "stylus"
    Driver         "wacom"
    Option         "Device" "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
    Option         "Type" "stylus"
    Option         "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
    Identifier     "eraser"
    Driver         "wacom"
    Option         "Device" "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
    Option         "Type" "eraser"
    Option         "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
    Identifier     "cursor"
    Driver         "wacom"
    Option         "Device" "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
    Option         "Type" "cursor"
    Option         "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
    Identifier     "DELL P1130"
    Option         "DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Device"
    Identifier     "NVIDIA Corporation NVIDIA Default Card"
    Driver         "nvidia"
    Option         "TwinView" "yes"
    Option         "RenderAccel"
    Option         "TwinViewOrientation" "CRT-0 LeftOf CRT-1"
    Option         "MetaModes" "1600x1200,1600x1200; 1280x1024,1280x1024; 1024x768,1024x768; 800x600,800x600"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
    Identifier     "Default Screen"
    Device         "NVIDIA Corporation NVIDIA Default Card"
    Monitor        "DELL P1130"
    DefaultDepth    24    
    Option         "HorizSync" "CRT-0: 30-130; CRT-1: 30-130"
    Option         "VertRefresh" "CRT-0: 48-170; CRT-1: 48-170"
    SubSection     "Display"
        Depth       1
        Modes      "1600x1200" "1280x1024" "1152x864" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
    EndSubSection
    SubSection     "Display"
        Depth       4
        Modes      "1600x1200" "1280x1024" "1152x864" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
    EndSubSection
    SubSection     "Display"
        Depth       8
        Modes      "1600x1200" "1280x1024" "1152x864" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
    EndSubSection
    SubSection     "Display"
        Depth       15
        Modes      "1600x1200" "1280x1024" "1152x864" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
    EndSubSection
    SubSection     "Display"
        Depth       16
        Modes      "1600x1200" "1280x1024" "1152x864" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
    EndSubSection
    SubSection     "Display"
        Depth       24
	Modes      "1600x1200" "1280x1024" "1152x864" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
    EndSubSection
EndSection



```
